sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: The cortex MCP server exposes 20 tools via the Model Context Protocol. All tools are implemented in `cortex/mcp_server.py` using the FastMCP framework. Each tool accepts `project_root` (str) as its first parameter.

sections.heading: cortex_sql
sections.content:
Run a read-only SQL query against the cortex index for debugging.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `query` | str | Yes | A SELECT SQL statement to execute |
| `max_rows` | int | No (default 50, max 500) | Maximum rows to return |

**Returns:** Formatted table of results. Only SELECT statements are allowed. Use for diagnostic queries against `nodes`, `node_history`, `edges`, `tags`, `node_verification`, `docs`, and `doc_sections` tables. Values longer than 80 characters are truncated. Returns `(no rows)` for empty results, `ERROR:` for non-SELECT queries.

sections.heading: cortex_build
sections.content:
Rebuild the cortex index by scanning source files (discovery-only). Optionally purge NOT_FOUND nodes.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | — | Absolute path to the project to index |
| `verbose` | bool | No | `False` | Include all warning details in output |
| `purge` | bool | No | `False` | After build, remove all nodes whose source files no longer exist on disk (NOT_FOUND nodes), along with their edges, tags, FTS entries, and history. Destructive. |

**Returns:** Summary of nodes/edges indexed, staleness counts, and purge results (if requested).

sections.heading: cortex_list
sections.content:
List indexed nodes with optional filters.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `node_type` | str | No | Filter by type: `atomic_process`, `composite_process`, `entity` (ADR-009: `document` and `constraint` retired) |
| `tag` | str | No | Filter to nodes with this tag |
| `parent_id` | str | No | Filter to direct children via `composes` edges — works for module nodes (returns functions) and doc file nodes (returns section nodes) |
| `location` | str | No | Filter by file path substring |

**Returns:** Formatted list of nodes with ID, type, and title.

sections.heading: cortex_render
sections.content:
Render a node at a specified detail level.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `node_id` | str | No | Full cortex node ID. Omit to render all nodes (paginated). |
| `level` | int | Yes | Detail level: 0=ids only, 1=summary+tags+staleness badge, 2=full docstring+staleness badge on header, 3=source excerpt |
| `max_results` | int | No (default 60) | Cap for batch rendering |
| `offset` | int | No (default 0) | Pagination offset |

**Returns:** Formatted text at the requested detail level. At levels 1 and 2, non-VERIFIED staleness badges are appended inline — e.g. `[CONTENT_UPDATED]`, `[LINKED_STALE]`, or `[NOT_FOUND]` (reads persisted two-column staleness directly, no computation). VERIFIED nodes receive no annotation (happy-path is noise-free).

sections.heading: cortex_graph
sections.content:
Get a node's neighborhood in the knowledge graph.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | Seed node ID |
| `depth` | int | No (default 1) | Number of hops from seed |
| `direction` | str | No (default 'both') | Edge direction: 'in', 'out', or 'both' |

**Returns:** List of connected nodes with edge types and directions.

sections.heading: cortex_search
sections.content:
Full-text and semantic search across all indexed nodes and doc sections.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | — | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `query` | str | Yes | — | Search query (natural language or identifiers) |
| `level` | int | No | None | Detail level for results (1 or 2) |
| `max_results` | int | No | 20 | Maximum results to return |
| `node_type` | str | No | None | Filter by node type |
| `mode` | str | No | `"keyword"` | Search mode: `keyword` (FTS5) or `semantic` (vector similarity) |
| `scope` | str | No | `"all"` | Result scope: `code` (code nodes only), `docs` (doc sections only), or `all` |
| `tag` | str | No | None | Only return nodes with this tag. Applied as post-filter after FTS ranking. When tag filter is active and no matches are found, returns empty instead of falling through to LIKE-OR fallback. |

**Keyword mode:** Uses 3-stage search: FTS5 MATCH, LIKE-AND, LIKE-OR. Returns results ranked by BM25. Tag filter is applied after each stage.

**Semantic mode:** Embeds the query using the local embedding model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384 dims) and returns nearest neighbors from the sqlite-vec virtual table. Falls back to keyword mode if sqlite-vec or embedding model is unavailable. Tag filter not yet supported in semantic mode.

**Returns:** Up to `max_results` results with ID, title, type, and snippet. Doc sections include their full qualified section ID.

sections.heading: cortex_source
sections.content:
Return the raw source body of a node, looked up by ID.

Uses `level_3_location` from the index to locate the file and line range, then returns those source lines directly.  Eliminates the need to manually parse file paths and line offsets after a `cortex_search`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | Full node ID, e.g. `myproject::pm.cli::some_function` |

**Returns:** Source lines prefixed with a `# {node_id}  @ {location}` header.  For module-level nodes (no line range), the entire file is returned.  Returns `ERROR:` string if the node is not found or has no location.

sections.heading: cortex_check
sections.content:
Compute staleness for all nodes via the shared staleness engine (ADR-009: single writer). Results stored as two orthogonal columns: `own_status` and `link_status`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `verbose` | bool | No (default `False`) | When `True`, include the full problem-node table. Default returns summary only. |

**Returns:** Always starts with a one-line summary counts line. Pass `verbose=True` to also get the full table of problem nodes.

Summary format: `{N} CONTENT_UPDATED · {N} DESC_UPDATED · {N} NOT_FOUND · {N} LINKED_STALE · {N} BROKEN_LINK · {N} VERIFIED`

own_status values (content vs verified baseline):
- **VERIFIED** — disk hashes match the verified baseline (was CLEAN).
- **CONTENT_UPDATED** — body hash changed since last verification (was CONTENT_UPDATED). Promotable via `cortex_mark_clean`.
- **DESC_UPDATED** — heading/docstring hash changed (was DESC_UPDATED). Promotable via `cortex_mark_clean`.
- **NOT_FOUND** — file or node no longer exists on disk (was NOT_FOUND). Cannot be marked clean.

link_status values (dependency health):
- **VERIFIED** — no linked dependency has changed since this node was last updated.
- **LINKED_STALE** — a documented/validated node changed after this node. Cannot be marked clean — must update the prose/test.
- **BROKEN_LINK** — outbound edge points to a non-existent node. Cannot be marked clean.

sections.heading: cortex_history
sections.content:
View the change history for a specific node.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | The node to inspect |
| `limit` | int | No (default 10) | Number of history entries to return (max 100) |

**Returns:** Timeline of changes with human-readable descriptions. Change types include:
- **Content events:** `INITIAL`, `CONTENT_ONLY`, `DESC_ONLY`, `CONTENT_AND_DESC`
- **Actor events:** `AGENT_VERIFIED`, `MANUAL_VERIFIED`, `CHECKPOINT`
- **State events:** `BECAME_CONTENT_UPDATED`, `BECAME_DESC_UPDATED`, `BECAME_LINKED_STALE`, `BECAME_NOT_FOUND`, `BECAME_BROKEN_LINK`, `BECAME_VERIFIED`
- **Structural events:** `LINK_ADDED`, `LINK_REMOVED`
- **Lifecycle events:** `DELETED`

Annotated with stale window duration for `CONTENT_ONLY` events that lack a subsequent description update. Commit subjects included for rows with a `git_sha`.

sections.heading: cortex_list_reference_points
sections.content:
List available reference points for `cortex_report(since_sha=...)`. Call this before `cortex_report` to discover valid SHA values.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |

**Returns:** `[N reference point(s)]` header, then one line per reference showing short SHA (12 chars), type (`CHECKPOINT` or build SHA), timestamp, row count, and checkpoint message (if any). Returns a guidance message suggesting `cortex build` or `cortex history checkpoint` if no reference points exist.

sections.heading: cortex_report
sections.content:
Impact report: what changed since a checkpoint, SHA, or datetime.

Summarises content changes, staleness transitions, link modifications, verification activity, and deleted nodes recorded in `node_history`.

**Reference point resolution** (first match wins):
1. `since_sha` — match a CHECKPOINT by git_sha prefix.
2. `since_sha` — match any history row by git_sha prefix (works after `cortex init` without requiring a checkpoint).
3. `since_timestamp` — ISO-8601 datetime used directly as cutoff.
4. Neither given — most recent CHECKPOINT, then most recent history row with a git_sha.
5. No reference found — report over entire history.

See the Since Filter section in the history design spec for the full resolution logic with examples.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `since_sha` | str | No | Git SHA prefix. Matched against history rows (CHECKPOINTs checked first, then any row). |
| `since_timestamp` | str | No | ISO-8601 datetime cutoff |
| `verbose` | bool | No (default `False`) | When `True`, include full breakdown by category. Default returns summary only |
| `change_type_pattern` | str | No | Glob pattern to filter change types (e.g. `*STALE*`, `LINK_*`, `AGENT_*`, `INITIAL`) |
| `node_pattern` | str | No | Glob pattern to filter node IDs (e.g. `cortex::cortex.viz.*`) |
| `node_type` | str | No | Filter to nodes of this type. One of `atomic_process`, `composite_process`, or `entity`. |

**Returns:** Always starts with a one-line summary (e.g. `5 nodes changed, 2 became stale, 3 verified (1 agent-only), 4 links modified`). Pass `verbose=True` for full breakdown by category (content changes, staleness transitions, link changes, verification activity).

sections.heading: cortex_diff
sections.content:
Show what changed in a node since a baseline commit. Supports both code nodes (line-range sliced source diff) and doc nodes (section-level diff via `get_doc_diff`).

**Baseline resolution** (when `baseline_sha` is omitted):
1. Most recent `AGENT_VERIFIED`, `MANUAL_VERIFIED`, or `CHECKPOINT` history row with a non-NULL `git_sha`.
2. Fallback: oldest history row with a `git_sha` (typically the `INITIAL` scan — gives "diff since first indexed").

Pass `baseline_sha` explicitly to diff against a specific commit (e.g. a SHA from `cortex_history` output or the "since" filter's resolved SHA).

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | The node to diff |
| `baseline_sha` | str | No | Git SHA to diff against. If omitted, auto-resolves from history |

**Returns:** JSON-formatted text with keys: `node_id`, `baseline_sha`, `baseline_date`, `old_content`, `new_content`, `summary` (line-count delta, e.g. "+5 / -2 lines in body"), `commit_subject`, `commit_author`, `commit_date`. Returns `{"error": "no_baseline", "reason": "..."}` if no baseline exists or `git show` fails.

sections.heading: cortex_mark_clean
sections.content:
Record an agent verification for a stale node. Operates on the own_status dimension only.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | Node to verify |
| `reason` | str | No | Why the docs are still accurate |

**Returns:** Confirmation message. Records AGENT_VERIFIED in node_verification table, storing both `code_hash_at` and `desc_hash_at`.

Only applicable to `CONTENT_UPDATED` and `DESC_UPDATED` nodes (own_status dimension). Link_status dimension (`LINKED_STALE`, `BROKEN_LINK`) cannot be marked clean — they must be resolved by updating the linked prose/test or rebuilding.

sections.heading: cortex_write_doc
sections.content:
Create or overwrite a DocJSON file and register it in the index.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `doc_json` | str | Yes | JSON string with keys: `title`, `sections` (required) and optionally `tags`. An `id` key, if present, is used only as a filename hint (slug derivation) and stripped before writing. Each section needs `id`, `heading` and optionally `content`, `links` (list of `{node_id: ...}`), `tags`. |

The file is written to `docs/{slug}.json` where the slug is derived from the `id` field (if present) or the `title`. The file is immediately scanned and indexed. `LINK_ADDED` history rows are recorded for each `documents` edge created from section links.

**Depth validation:** Rejects documents with section nesting exceeding 3 levels (depth 0, 1, 2) with an ERROR message.

**Returns:** Summary with file path written, sections written count, links registered count, and any unknown node_id warnings.

sections.heading: cortex_add_link
sections.content:
Add a `documents` link from a doc section to a code node. Writes the link into the DocJSON file and re-indexes.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `section_id` | str | Yes | Full section ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture::database-layer` |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | Target code node ID to link to |

**Returns:** Confirmation message.

**Section lookup:** The section slug (the part after the last `::`) supports dot-path notation for nested sections. For example, `myproject::docs.arch::database-layer.tables` targets the `tables` child section inside `database-layer`. The lookup recursively traverses the JSON `sections` tree following the dot-separated path.

**History:** Records a `LINK_ADDED` history row on the source (section) node for each new link.

sections.heading: cortex_update_section
sections.content:
Update a single section's content, heading, and/or ID in a DocJSON file. The caller provides plain markdown — no need to construct full DocJSON. Reads the file, patches the target section, writes back, and re-indexes.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `section_id` | str | Yes | Full section ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture::database-layer` |
| `content` | str | No | New markdown content for the section |
| `heading` | str | No | New heading for the section |
| `new_id` | str | No | New section ID (simple slug, lowercase + hyphens only). Renames the section and cascades through all DB references (doc_sections, edges, history). Children of a renamed parent have their IDs cascaded automatically. |

At least one of `content`, `heading`, or `new_id` must be provided.

**Returns:** Confirmation of which fields were updated, including old → new ID mapping if renamed.

**Section lookup:** The section slug (the part after the last `::`) supports dot-path notation for nested sections. For example, `myproject::docs.arch::database-layer.tables` targets the `tables` child section inside `database-layer`. The lookup recursively traverses the JSON `sections` tree following the dot-separated path.

sections.heading: cortex_add_section
sections.content:
Add a section to an existing DocJSON document without rewriting the whole file. Loads the JSON, inserts the new section object at the specified position, writes back, and re-indexes.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `doc_id` | str | Yes | Full doc node ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture` |
| `section_id` | str | Yes | Slug for the new section (e.g. `"migrations"`) |
| `heading` | str | Yes | Heading text for the new section |
| `content` | str | No | Markdown content (defaults to empty) |
| `after` | str | No | Sibling section slug to insert after. Appends to end if omitted |
| `parent_id` | str | No | Dot-path of the parent section for nesting (e.g. `"database-layer"`). Omit for top-level |

**Returns:** Confirmation with the full section ID of the new section.

**Depth validation:** Rejects insertions that would exceed max nesting depth (3 levels: depth 0, 1, 2).

sections.heading: cortex_delete_section
sections.content:
Delete a section (and all nested children) from a DocJSON document. Destructive.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `section_id` | str | Yes | Full qualified section ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture::database-layer` or `myproject::docs.architecture::database-layer.tables` |

**Behavior:** Removes the section from the JSON file on disk, then cleans up all DB rows (nodes, edges, doc_sections, tags, FTS entries, verification). Node history is preserved as an audit trail. Nested children are deleted recursively.

**Returns:** Confirmation message or error if section not found.

sections.heading: cortex_delete_doc
sections.content:
Delete an entire DocJSON document, its JSON file, and all DB artifacts. Destructive.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `doc_id` | str | Yes | Full doc node ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture` |

**Behavior:** Deletes the JSON file from disk, then calls `delete_doc_by_id` which removes all nodes, edges, sections, tags, FTS entries, and history for the doc. No confirmation prompt.

**Returns:** Confirmation message or error if doc not found.

sections.heading: cortex_delete_link
sections.content:
Remove a single `documents` edge from a doc section to a code node. Destructive.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `section_id` | str | Yes | Full qualified section ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture::database-layer` |
| `node_id` | str | Yes | The code node ID to unlink |

**Behavior:** Removes the link from the JSON file, re-indexes the document, and records a `LINK_REMOVED` history event. Other links and content in the section are untouched.

**Returns:** Confirmation message, or "Link not found" if the edge does not exist.

sections.heading: cortex_list_tags
sections.content:
List all distinct tags in the index with node counts. Useful for discovering available tags before filtering with `cortex_list(tag=...)` or `cortex_search(tag=...)`.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |

**Returns:** Table of tags and their node counts, or "No tags found in index." if empty.

sections.heading: cortex_read_doc
sections.content:
Read a DocJSON file as Markdown. Each section heading is annotated with its full section ID in an HTML comment, enabling direct use in `cortex_update_section` or `cortex_add_link` without a separate lookup.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `doc_id` | str | Yes | Full doc node ID, e.g. `myproject::docs.architecture`. Pass `"list"` to enumerate all doc IDs |
| `section` | str | No | Short slug to read a single section, e.g. `"problem"` or `"architecture"`. Matched against the tail of each section ID (part after last `::`). If multiple sections match, all are returned with a disambiguation header listing full section IDs. Omit to read the full document |

**Returns:** Rendered Markdown. If `section` is provided and multiple sections match, output begins with `[N sections matched '{slug}' — returning all. Use full section_id in cortex_update_section to be specific.]` followed by the matched section IDs and their content.

sections.heading: cortex_list_undocumented
sections.content:
Find nodes that have no associated documentation.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | No | Project directory |
| `node_type` | str | No | Filter by node type |

**Returns:** List of nodes that have no `documents` edges pointing to them.

sections.heading: cortex_workflow_list
sections.content:
List dFlow workflow, task, and test_workflow functions. Returns one line per function with name, decorator role, purpose summary, file location, and the corresponding cortex node ID.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `module` | str | No | File path substring filter, e.g. `"staleness"` returns only workflows in files containing that string |
| `role` | str | No | Filter by decorator type: `"workflow"`, `"task"`, or `"test_workflow"`. Omit to list all roles |
| `max_results` | int | No (default 30) | Maximum rows returned |
| `offset` | int | No (default 0) | Pagination offset |

**Returns:** `[N of M workflows]` header, then one line per function. Each line includes the cortex node ID (when resolved) so agents can cross into `cortex_render` / `cortex_graph` / `cortex_source`. Returns a graceful message when no dFlow database is present.

**Data source:** Queries `.dflow/workflow.db` directly (no import of dFlow). Cross-references cortex.db for node ID resolution.

sections.heading: cortex_workflow_detail
sections.content:
Show ordered steps and metadata for a single dFlow workflow or task function.

**Parameters:**
| Name | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `project_root` | str | Yes | Absolute path to the indexed project |
| `workflow_id` | str | Yes | Function name (e.g. `"run_pipeline"`) or dFlow integer ID from `cortex_workflow_list` |
| `verbose` | bool | No (default `False`) | When `True`, include `purpose`, `inputs`, `outputs`, and `critical` fields on both the workflow header and each step |

**Returns:** Header with function name, role, file location, and cortex node ID. Followed by ordered steps with step number, name, and delegate target (cortex node ID when resolved). For `@test_workflow` functions, also lists coverage targets (validates links). With `verbose=True`, each step includes purpose, inputs, outputs, and critical fields.

**Data source:** Queries `.dflow/workflow.db` directly. Cross-references cortex.db for node ID resolution.
